title: Environmental DNA (eDNA) COI metabarcoding Illumina MiSeq NGS PCR Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"e2k6d","text":"This protocol is aimed at amplifying the cytochrome c oxidase subunit I (COI) mitochondrial gene in eukaryotes. The primers (forward: mlCOIintF, reverse: HCO2198) utilized in this protocol are based on the primers utilized in Leray et al. 2013 (forward) and Folmer et al. 1994 (reverse).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1uvf6","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"detmt","text":"Amplicons generated using this protocol can then be sequenced using the Illumina platform.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5loia","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"40b1m","text":"Primers used:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1pc6s","text":"Fluidigm CS1+mlCOIinfF","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"44beg","text":"Fluidigm CS2+HCO2198","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aa11f","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d56ed","text":"Secondary COI PCR primers","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8iv7q","text":"PE1-BC-CS1","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3ur5n","text":"PE2-BC-CS2 \n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines:
{"blocks":[{"key":"elu0f","text":" Local containment involves the employment of biological safety cabinets for initial specimen disinfection and DNA extraction. Such cabinets should not be used for PCR setup, as this procedure should be conducted apart from the aforementioned disinfection and extraction to minimize contamination.
  
  Do not use any tube or plate that is not appropriate for the PCR machine you are using.
 Make sure tubes and especially plates are well sealed before you begin run.
 Clean up any spilled solutions and dispose of in appropriate biohazard boxes.
 Be careful with PCR machine lids. These can be damaged if you slam or drop lids.
 Make sure PCR heater block is clean before you start a run. Check each tube receptacle before you start.
 Distribute tubes evenly across block so lid will seat flat against top of tubes for even heating and sealing.
 Turn PCR machine off when you are done using.
  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":57,"length":6},{"style":"italic","offset":111,"length":3},{"style":"italic","offset":164,"length":3},{"style":"italic","offset":183,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
creator: Reiko P Michisaki
